prompt:
The after-school coding club wants a referee for Go Fish tournaments — every table plays the same stacked deck and the same asks, so results are comparable. I need a single script `gofish.rb`, stdlib only, run as `ruby gofish.rb `. It replays the game and prints the whole event log to stdout, one event per line, exactly as spec'd below (our diff tool is unforgiving). Cards are rank+suit: ranks A 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 T J Q K (that canonical order matters everywhere ranks are sorted), suits C D H S. Deck file: one card per line, top of the stock first. Reject bad lines (`error: bad deck line : `), duplicates (`error: duplicate card `), decks needn't be full 52 — small test decks are a thing. Players are seats P1..Pn. CLI validation, all errors on stderr with exit 2, in this order: wrong arg count -> `usage: gofish.rb `; players not an integer 2-4 -> `error: players must be 2-4`; cards-each not an integer >= 1 -> `error: cards-each must be >= 1`; unreadable deck or script file -> `error: cannot read `; then deck line/duplicate checks; finally if the deck can't cover the deal -> `error: not enough cards`. Setup: deal one card at a time round-robin starting at P1 (P1 gets line 1, P2 line 2, ...). Then, per player in seat order: lay down every completed book (all 4 of a rank) as `BOOK

` in canonical rank order, and — the club's redraw rule — any player whose hand is empty immediately draws ONE card from the stock if it has any: `REDRAW

`. If the game isn't already over, print `TURN P1`. The script file holds the asks: each line ` ` applies to whoever's turn it is (blank lines, whitespace-only lines and `#` comments are skipped). A line that isn't exactly a P-number token plus a valid rank token prints `ILLEGAL bad line: ` (line stripped of surrounding whitespace); then, checked in this order: target missing from the table or equal to the asker -> `ILLEGAL cannot ask `; target has no cards -> `ILLEGAL has no cards`; asker doesn't hold the rank -> `ILLEGAL does not hold `. Illegal lines never consume the turn. A legal ask prints `ASK `, then: if the target holds any of that rank they hand over ALL of them (`GIVE x`) and the asker keeps the turn. Otherwise `GOFISH `: with stock remaining the asker draws the top card (`DRAW `), and if the drawn rank equals the asked rank that's `LUCKY ` and the turn stays; with an empty stock print `STOCK EMPTY`. Any time cards enter a hand, completed books are laid immediately (`BOOK`), and the empty-hand redraw rule applies the moment a hand empties — event order after a give is: GIVE, giver's REDRAW if they just emptied, asker's BOOK, asker's REDRAW; after a draw: DRAW, BOOK, REDRAW, then LUCKY if it applies. Turn passing: when the turn moves on (a miss, or the asker kept the turn but ended up with no cards — booking with a dry stock does that), advance clockwise to the next seat, announcing `SKIP

` for anyone with an empty hand along the way, then `TURN

`. The game ends the instant every hand AND the stock are empty: print `GAME OVER`. If the script runs out first: `SCRIPT END`. Either way finish with one `FINAL

` per seat — ranks comma-joined in canonical order, or `-` for none — and exit 0. Fixture deck/script for a full 3-seat game live in `fixtures/`. `ruby test_gofish.rb` is the acceptance suite and pins whole transcripts including that full game; don't edit the tests or fixtures.
